other_observers: Nancy Gobris; Nancy Gobris found and identified the bird on 13 May 2004; Brandon Percival saw it on that date, too.
specific_location: at the banding station in the Holmes Grove on Chico Basin Ranch
description: A distinctive warbler, the bird had a black forehead, with the black bleeding up onto the forecrown. The rest of the crown, and the upperparts down through the rump were blue-gray. It had a distinct and well-defined white superciliary with a hint of yellow in the supraloral part of that feature. There was an obvious black eyeline that connected to black auriculars. A single white eye arc was positioned under each eye and the area immediately behind the auriculars was white. The chin, throat, and upper breast were bright yellow, the rest of the underparts being white with the sides have bold, black streaking extending onto the flanks.  The wings and tail were dark, with the wings being marked by bold, white wingbars (tips to the greater and median coverts) and blue-gray edges to most feathers; and with the inner webs of the outer 4-5 pairs of rectrices being primarily bright white and forming large, obvious tail spots.Behaviors: foraging on the trunks and larger branches of cottonwoods, somewhat like a creeper or nuthatch; also perched for a while on a power line and foraged on the roof of the shed at the banding stationCall: It sang once while I was present (more when I wasn't present) -- a series of sliding whistles that descended in pitch until the end when it ascended again.Plumage: Alternate
